items.front: What departure information must be issued to departing aircraft as appropriate?
items.back: Issue runway, wind, altimeter, time if requested, below-minima ceiling and visibility, taxi route, braking action, RwyCC, and a shortened-runway advisory when required and ATIS is unavailable.

items.front: What delay information is given when gate-hold procedures are in effect?
items.back: Advise when engine start or taxi can be expected, when to start or advise ready to taxi, approve delay-area requests if traffic permits, and announce when gate hold ends.

items.front: What departure-control instructions are issued before and after takeoff?
items.back: Before takeoff, issue departure frequency and beacon code unless the SID publishes the frequency. After takeoff, switch aircraft when appropriate, but do not require military turboprop or turbojet changes before 2,500.

items.front: What are the key rules for line up and wait (LUAW)?
items.back: Use LUAW only for an imminent departure, state the runway first, issue traffic, avoid conditional phrases, and do not use it where the departure point or intersection cannot be verified or where restrictions prohibit it.

items.front: When may takeoff separation be anticipated?
items.back: A takeoff clearance may be issued before full separation exists if there is reasonable assurance the required separation will exist when takeoff roll begins.

items.front: What is the core same-runway departure separation rule?
items.back: Do not let the aircraft begin takeoff roll until the preceding departure has crossed the runway end or turned away, or the preceding arrival is clear. Apply the required category-based wake intervals when applicable.

items.front: How is wake-turbulence separation applied to intersection departures?
items.back: Apply the required time intervals before the intersection departure starts takeoff roll, give a hold-for-wake advisory when needed, and if a waiver is used, issue a wake caution and required deviation clearance.

items.front: When may a departure begin takeoff roll on an intersecting runway?
items.back: Only after traffic information is exchanged and the other aircraft has passed the intersection or flight path, is clear or holding short as required, or LAHSO protection applies. Required wake intervals still apply.

items.front: When may a departure begin on a nonintersecting converging runway?
items.back: Only after the other aircraft has crossed or turned away, or the arriving aircraft will hold short, has passed the projected intersection, or has crossed the departure runway. Apply wake intervals if the paths cross.

items.front: What must a takeoff clearance include?
items.back: State the runway number first, then the takeoff clearance. Include the intersection, full-length or cross-runway wording when required, and say "shortened" whenever runway length has been reduced.

items.front: When may a takeoff clearance be canceled?
items.back: Cancel it and give the reason whenever needed before roll. After takeoff roll starts, cancel only for safety, not for traffic-management reasons alone.
